steps.title: Use a one-parameter regression shortcut
steps.content:
The target form is $9x^4+x^3+ax^2-x$, so only $a$ is unknown. In Desmos, make a table for the original expression and fit it to that exact form:
$$x_1=[-2,-1,0,1,2]$$
$$y_1=(9x_1^4+8x_1^3)-(7x_1^3+5x_1^2)-(3x_1^2+x_1)$$
$$y_1\sim 9x_1^4+x_1^3+ax_1^2-x_1$$

steps.title: Read the value of a
steps.content:
Desmos returns $a=-8$. That means the expression is:
$$9x^4+x^3-8x^2-x$$

steps.title: Confirm with algebra
steps.content:
Distribute the negative signs:
$$(9x^4+8x^3)-(7x^3+5x^2)-(3x^2+x)=9x^4+8x^3-7x^3-5x^2-3x^2-x$$
Combine like terms: $8x^3-7x^3=x^3$ and $-5x^2-3x^2=-8x^2$.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: The coefficient of $x^2$ is $a=-8$, which matches choice A. Therefore A is correct.
